results.title: Linear combinations, span, and basis vectors | Chapter 2, Essence of linear algebra
results.reason: Widely regarded as the gold standard for visual intuition, this video explains the 'why' behind span and basis vectors using high-quality animations.
summary: How to scale and add two different vectors
The collection of all possible reachable points
Using basic building blocks to reach any spot
What happens when vectors line up or overlap

results.title: Linear transformations and matrices | Chapter 3, Essence of linear algebra
results.reason: The creator successfully recontextualizes matrices as geometric transformations, a shift that viewers frequently cite as a 'lightbulb moment' for beginners.
summary: Moving space while keeping grid lines straight
How matrices act like functions for moving points
Tracking where the basic unit vectors land
Using numbers to describe a visual grid change

results.title: Eigenvectors and eigenvalues | Chapter 14, Essence of linear algebra
results.reason: While slightly more advanced (Chapter 14), it provides the most intuitive explanation of eigenvectors available, though beginners should watch earlier chapters first.
summary: Finding special lines that do not rotate
Vectors that only stretch or shrink during change
The number that shows how much they stretch
Why these special vectors simplify complex math problems

results.title: The Big Picture of Linear Algebra
results.reason: Features Gilbert Strang, a legend in the field; while highly authoritative, his 'big picture' approach is often better appreciated after a few introductory lessons.
summary: How four main spaces connect in one map
Solving equations by looking at column combinations
Understanding the inputs that result in zero
Seeing how rows and columns relate to each other

results.title: Dear linear algebra students, This is what matrices (and matrix manipulation) really look like
results.reason: Zach Star excels at connecting abstract matrix manipulation to physical reality, which viewers find extremely helpful for grounding their understanding.
summary: Visualizing matrix math as a physical transformation
Why we multiply rows by columns in order
Seeing how data changes shape through a matrix
A better way to imagine abstract math rules
